content.text: Found it. Your "Move to Stanford" commit (`c499730`) is a dangling commit — you checked out `HEAD~1` (detached HEAD), committed there, then returned to master, orphaning it. Let me inspect it before merging.

content.text: The commit is a genuine bio update (PhD student → Stanford Postdoc). It was based on `c4e38a1`, one commit behind master's tip `d7d3e4b` ("off the job market woo"). Let me check what that master commit changed, since both may touch `about.md`.

content.text: Both commits edit the same lines of `about.md`, so a cherry-pick will conflict there. The Stanford commit is the intended newer content, so I'll resolve that file to the Stanford version; `default.html` only changes in the Stanford commit and applies cleanly.

content.text:
Done. Your changes are recovered and merged into master.

**What happened:** You had checked out `HEAD~1` (a detached HEAD), committed your "Move to Stanford" edits there as `c499730`, then checked out master — which left that commit dangling with no branch pointing to it, so it "disappeared."

**What I did:**
- Found the orphaned commit `c499730` ("Move to Stanford") via the reflog / `git fsck`.
- Cherry-picked it onto master. It conflicted in `_includes/about.md` because master's tip (`d7d3e4b`, "off the job market woo") had edited the same lines. I resolved to your Stanford version — bio now reads "Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS" with the job-search line removed. The `_layouts/default.html` changes (Stanford header, font/formatting cleanup) applied cleanly.
- Master is now at `0b60363` with a clean working tree.

**Remaining step:** this is local only — run `git push` when you're ready to publish. Nothing was lost; the original dangling commit still exists in the reflog if you ever need it.
